page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz with a single thread per core. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and utilizing a KVM hypervisor. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 32 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 62125.29 and a Passmark CPU score of 55792.37, both placing in the top 25 percent. Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1861.29. Cryptographic operations perform well, as shown by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 51982.03. Memory bandwidth tests show average read-only speeds of 168.6 GB/sec for large blocks and write speeds of 68.03 GB/sec. For language model tasks, the instance achieves strong prompt processing speeds of 2455.35 tokens per second on small 135M models, though performance scales down to an average of 105.0 tokens per second for medium 7B models.

The instance offers a high density of dedicated compute resources relative to its memory capacity, making it a cost-efficient option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications, and the lack of local storage requires reliance on network storage. This hardware profile is appropriate for multi-threaded batch processing, cryptographic workloads, web serving, database operations, and small-scale model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores running at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, it provides a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation, with no local storage or GPUs. The instance operates on a KVM hypervisor and delivers a 32 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 62125.29 and a Passmark CPU score of 55792.37. It also demonstrates strong cryptographic capabilities and efficient small-model LLM inference. The 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio makes it cost-efficient for compute-heavy tasks, though it presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. It is suited for multi-threaded processing, database operations, and web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB RAM, and 32 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.16xlarge features 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 128 GB RAM, optimized for compute-heavy workloads and multi-threaded processing.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core compute-optimized instance with high multi-threaded performance and 32 Gbps network bandwidth for CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores running at 2.7 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing a 2.0 GB per core allocation
32 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
No local storage or integrated GPU resources bundled
Appropriate for multi-threaded batch processing and database operations
